\textit{Definitions.} For a positive integer $n$, let $v_p(n)$ denote the largest integer $v$ such that $p^v \mid n$.

For $p$ a prime and $a \not\equiv 0 \pmod{p}$, we let $\ord_p(a)$ denote the smallest positive integer $o$ such that $a^o \equiv 1 \pmod{p}$. For $x > 0$, we let
$$\ord_{p, x}(a) = \prod_{q \le x} q^{v_q(\ord_p(a))} \prod_{q > x} q^{v_q(p-1)}.$$

\textit{Problem.} Let $S_x$ denote the set of primes $p$ for which
$$\ord_{p, x}(2) > \ord_{p, x}(3),$$
and let $d_x$ denote the density
$$d_x := \frac{|S_x|}{|\{p \le x : p \text{ is prime}|\}}$$
of $S_x$ in the primes. Let
$$d_{\infty} = \lim_{x \to \infty} d_x.$$

Compute $\lfloor 10^6 d_{\infty} \rfloor.$

How many nonzero points are there on
\[x^3y+y^3z+z^3x=0\]

over $\mathbb{F}_{5^{18}}$ up to scaling?

Let $a_n$ for $n\in\mathbb Z$ be the sequence of integers satisfying the recurrence formula 
   \[a_n=198130309625 a_{n-1}+354973292077 a_{n-2}-427761277677 a_{n-3}+370639957a_{n-4}\]
    with initial conditions $a_i=i$ for $0\leq i\leq 3$. Find the smallest prime $p\equiv4\bmod7$ for which the function $\mathbb Z\to\mathbb Z$ given by $n\mapsto a_n$ can be extended to a continuous function on $\mathbb Z_p$.

There are unique constants $C, \alpha, \beta$ such that the number of solutions to the equation

$$ab+1=cde$$

with $a, b, c, d, e \in \mathbb{N}$ with $ab \le x$ is asymptotic to $Cx^{\alpha}\log^{\beta}x$ as $x\rightarrow\infty.$

Compute $\lfloor 1000C\rfloor.$

Let $U\subset\mathbb{P}H^0(\mathbb{P}^2_{\mathbb{Z}}, \mathcal{O}(2))$ be the space of smooth conics in $\mathbb{P}^2_{\mathbb{Z}}$, and let
$Z\subset U^6$ be the closed subscheme parametrizing $6$-tuples $(C_1, \cdots, C_6)$ with $C_1$ tangent to $C_2, \cdots, C_6$. Let $\pi: Z\to C^5$
be the map induced by the projection onto the last $5$ coordinates, and let $V\subset U^5$ be the dense open subscheme over which $\pi$ is finite \'etale.
Let $$L=\lim_{p\to \infty} \frac{1}{\#V(\mathbb{F}_p)}\sum_{x\in V(\mathbb{F}_p)} \#\pi^{-1}(x),$$
that is, the limit of the average number of components of the space of conics tangent to 5 smooth conics over $\mathbb{F}_p$, as $p$ tends to infinity (when this space is finite \'etale).

Find $\lfloor 100L \rfloor.$

\textit{Background.} 
The \emph{Tsirelson space} is a Banach space introduced in 1974 as a counterexample to a longstanding conjecture in Banach space theory. It is a subspace of $\ell^\infty$ described as follows. 

Let $P_k$ denote the operator on $\ell^\infty$ that modifies each $\langle x_n:\, n \in \mathbb N \rangle \in \ell^\infty$ by setting the first $k$ coordinates equal to $0$ (without changing any other coordinates). Given $\vec x = \langle x_n:\, n \in \mathbb N \rangle \in \ell^\infty$, the \emph{support} of $\vec x$ is $\mathrm{supp}(\vec x) = \{ n \in \mathbb N :\, x_n \neq 0 \}$. 
The Tsirelson space is the span of the smallest pointwise-closed subset $K$ of the unit ball in $\ell^\infty$ satisfying the following two properties:

1. For every $j \in \mathbb N$, the unit vector $\vec e_j$ and all its multiples $\lambda \vec e_j$ with $|\lambda| \leq 1$ belong to $K$.

2. Suppose $\vec x_1,\dots,\vec x_k$ is a finite sequence of members of $K$, each with finite support, such that $\max (\mathrm{supp}(\vec x_i)) < \min (\mathrm{supp}(\vec x_j))$ for all $i < j \leq k$. Then $\frac{1}{2}P_k(\vec x_1 + \dots + \vec x_k) \in K$.

\textit{Problem.}

Let $N$ denote the largest natural number such that 
there is a member $\langle x_n:\, n \in \mathbb N \rangle$ of the set $K$ described above with 
$x_3 = 1/2$, $x_N > 1/20$, and 
$x_i > x_j$ whenever $3 \leq i < j \leq N$. 
It turns out that $N$ is too large to write down in a reasonable amount of space, so let us ask instead: what is the largest power of $2$ dividing $N$? In other words, find the integer $p \geq 0$ such that $N = m2^p$ for some odd number $m$.

Let $W$ be the set of finite words with all distinct letters over the alphabet of positive integers. Define a function $F:W\rightarrow W$ recursively as follows. First, let $F(\epsilon)=\epsilon$, where $\epsilon$ is the empty word. Given a nonempty word $w\in W$, let $F(w)=F(L)F(R)m$, where $m$ is the largest number in $w$ and $w=LmR$. Let $n=10^{12}$, and note that $W$ restricts to a function from $S_n$ to itself, where $S_n$ is the symmetric group of order $n$. Let $\sigma$ be a permutation chosen uniformly at random from $S_n$, and let $X$ be the expected number of integers $1\le i<n$ such that $F(\sigma)(i+1)<F(\sigma)(i)$. Compute $\lfloor X\rfloor$.

Let $M_{1000}^4$ be the set of $4$-tuples of invertible $1000\times1000$ matrices with coefficients in $\mathbb{C}$. Let $S\subset M_{1000}^4$ be the subset of all tuples $(A_1,A_2,A_3,A_4)$ satisfying the conditions:
\begin{alignat*}{2}
A_i^2 &= I, &&\quad \text{for all } 1\le i\le 4\\
A_iA_j &= A_jA_i, &&\quad \text{if } \{3j- i, 3i-j\} \cap 5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} =\emptyset \\
A_iA_jA_i^{-1}A_j^{-1} &= A_jA_i, &&\quad \text{if } \{3j- i, 3i-j\} \cap 5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} \neq\emptyset,
\end{alignat*}
where $5\mathbb{Z}_{>0}$ refers to the set of positive multiples of $5$, i.e., $5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} = \{5,10,15,\dots\}$.

\noindent The group $G = GL(1000)$ of invertible complex $1000\times 1000$ matrices acts on $S$ by the formula:
\[B\cdot (A_1,A_2,A_3,A_4) = (BA_1B^{-1}, BA_2B^{-1}, BA_3B^{-1}, BA_4B^{-1}).\]
Find the number of orbits of this action, i.e., find $|S/G|$.

Let $\mathcal P$ be a regular $101$-gon of circumradius $1$. Draw each diagonal of $\mathcal P$ with probability 0.001. This splits $\mathcal P$ into several closed regions. Let $E$ be the expected value of the perimeter of the region containing the center of $\mathcal P$.

Compute $\lfloor 10^9 E \rfloor.$

Construct a degree 19 polynomial $p(x) \in \mathbb{C}[x]$ such that $X:=\{p(x) = p(y)\} \subset \mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1$ has at least 3 (but not all linear) irreducible components over $\mathbb{C}$. Choose $p(x)$ to be odd, monic, have real coefficients and linear coefficient -19 and calculate $p(19)$.

def answer():
    import math, random
    import numpy as np
    random.seed(0)
    np.random.seed(0)
    n = 101
    # Precompute vertices of the regular 101-gon with circumradius 1
    vertices = [(math.cos(2*math.pi*i/n), math.sin(2*math.pi*i/n)) for i in range(n)]
    # initial polygon P
    P = vertices[:]  
    # Precompute all diagonals (draw chord from vertex i to vertex (i+d) for d=2,...,(n+1)//2 - if n is odd, d=2,...,50)
    chords = []
    for i in range(n):
        for d in range(2, (n+1)//2):
            j = (i + d) % n
            chords.append((vertices[i], vertices[j]))
    nd = len(chords)  # should be 101*49 = 4949
    p = 0.001
    N_iter = 100000
    # center
    cx, cy = 0.0, 0.0
    def cross(ax, ay, bx, by):
        return ax*by - ay*bx
    def clip_polygon(poly, A, B):
        # clip polygon poly (list of (x,y)) by the half-plane determined by directed line A->B
        # The half-plane kept is the one that contains the center (0,0).
        (Ax, Ay) = A
        (Bx, By) = B
        # Determine sign for center: compute cross((B-A),(center-A))
        cp_center = cross(Bx - Ax, By - Ay, cx - Ax, cy - Ay)
        # if cp_center >= 0, then inside(x) should return True if cross(B-A, x-A) >= 0
        # otherwise, inside if cross(B-A, x-A) <= 0
        def inside(pt):
            x, y = pt
            cp = cross(Bx - Ax, By - Ay, x - Ax, y - Ay)
            if cp_center >= 0:
                return cp >= -1e-12
            else:
                return cp <= 1e-12
        def compute_intersection(p1, p2):
            # line p1->p2 and line A->B, compute intersection
            x1, y1 = p1
            x2, y2 = p2
            # Represent p1+p*(p2-p1) intersects A+q*(B-A)
            # Solve for p: cross(B-A, A - p1) / cross(B-A, p2 - p1)
            denom = cross(Bx - Ax, By - Ay, x2 - x1, y2 - y1)
            if abs(denom) < 1e-12:
                return p1
            t = cross(Bx - Ax, By - Ay, Ax - x1, Ay - y1)/denom
            return (x1 + t*(x2 - x1), y1 + t*(y2 - y1))
        new_poly = []
        nvert = len(poly)
        for i in range(nvert):
            curr = poly[i]
            nxt = poly[(i+1)%nvert]
            curr_in = inside(curr)
            nxt_in = inside(nxt)
            if curr_in and nxt_in:
                new_poly.append(nxt)
            elif curr_in and not nxt_in:
                inter = compute_intersection(curr, nxt)
                new_poly.append(inter)
            elif not curr_in and nxt_in:
                inter = compute_intersection(curr, nxt)
                new_poly.append(inter)
                new_poly.append(nxt)
            # if both out, add nothing
        return new_poly
    def polygon_perimeter(poly):
        per = 0.0
        for i in range(len(poly)):
            x1, y1 = poly[i]

    total_per = 0.0
    for _ in range(N_iter):

        if not region or len(region) < 3:

verification_code.answer:
```python
def answer():
    import math
    
    # We'll integrate over the fundamental domain [0, 2*pi/101], then multiply by 101.
    # For a regular 101-gon, the side constraints (support function) in direction theta:
    # For theta in [0, 2*pi/101], the active polygon constraint is:
    # if theta in [0, pi/101], then the nearest side normal is 0, and h_poly = cos(pi/101)/cos(theta).
    # if theta in [pi/101, 2*pi/101], then the nearest side normal is 2*pi/101, and h_poly = cos(pi/101)/cos(2*pi/101 - theta).
    
    def h_poly(theta):
        # theta in [0, 2*pi/101]
        half = math.pi/101
        if theta <= half:
            return math.cos(math.pi/101)/math.cos(theta)
        else:
            return math.cos(math.pi/101)/math.cos(2*math.pi/101 - theta)
    
    # For the drawn chords: for each chord type d from 2 to 50, and for each d, the effective constraint in the fundamental
    # domain comes from the unique value of k such that the chord normal mod (2*pi/101) falls in [0, 2*pi/101].
    # In fact, chord normals are of the form: 2*pi*k/101 + pi*d/101. Modulo 2*pi/101, they are all congruent to (pi*d/101) mod (2*pi/101).
    # Define for each d in {2,...,50}:
    # if d is even, then (pi*d/101) mod (2*pi/101) = 0, and if d is odd, then = pi/101.
    # Then the threshold value is: r_d(theta) = cos(pi*d/101) / cos(|theta - c_d|), where c_d is 0 for even, pi/101 for odd.
    
    def thresholds(theta):
        res = []
        for d in range(2, 51):
            c = 0.0 if (d % 2 == 0) else (math.pi/101)
            # Compute angular difference between theta and c (take absolute difference, but they lie in [0, 2*pi/101])
            diff = abs(theta - c)
            # For safety, ensure diff <= pi/2 (it always will be because 2*pi/101 ~ 0.062 means max diff is ~0.031 if c=pi/101)
            if diff < math.pi/2:
                val = math.cos(math.pi*d/101) / math.cos(diff)
                res.append(val)
        return sorted(res)
    
    # For a given theta, the support function in that direction for the random central region is the minimum of h_poly(theta) and all drawn chord constraints
    # that occur. A drawn chord appears with probability p = 0.001, independently for each chord type d (only one effective chord per d in the fundamental domain).
    # Thus, for each d in {2,...,50}, the constraint r_d is imposed with probability 0.001 and not imposed with probability 0.999.
    # And these are independent over d. The minimum over the constraints (only considering those that are imposed) is the minimum of the r_d among d for which the chord is drawn.
    # The survival function of the minimum is a product over d of factors that are step functions. Since the constraint if imposed is r_d, the effect is:
    # For a given theta and a given d, the contribution to the event that the minimum is > r is:
    # - if r < r_d, then even if the chord is drawn (with probability 0.001) it does not force the minimum to drop below r; if not drawn, it's fine.
    # - if r >= r_d, then if the chord is drawn, it forces the min to be <= r.
    # Thus, for each d, the probability that it does not force the support function to be <= r is 1 if r < r_d, and 0.999 if r >= r_d.
    # And since these are independent for each d, and the polygon constraint always gives an upper bound h_poly(theta),
    # we have: P(h(theta) > r) = 1_{r < h_poly(theta)} * prod_{d in D} [ (1 if r < r_d) + 0.999*(1 if r >= r_d) ].
    # But the ordering of constraints matters. If we order the thresholds r_d in increasing order: t_1, t_2, ..., t_m (m = number of d, which is 49)
    # then for r in [t_j, t_{j+1}), exactly j constraints are active (i.e. r >= t_i for i=1..j), and the product is 0.999^j.
    # Also, for r < t_1, product=1.
    # And for r >= t_m, product = 0.999^m, up until h_poly(theta). 
    # So the expected support function in direction theta is E[h(theta)] = \int_0^(h_poly(theta)) P(h(theta) > r) dr = \sum_{j=0}^{m} (interval length) * 0.999^j,
    # where the intervals are determined by the thresholds that are below h_poly(theta). 
    
    def E_h(theta, thr):
        # thr is the sorted list of thresholds for this theta
        hP = h_poly(theta)
        # Filter thresholds to only those below hP
        t = [x for x in thr if x < hP]
        # Add endpoints 0 and hP
        t = [0.0] + t + [hP]
        val = 0.0
        # For each interval [t[i], t[i+1]), the product factor is 0.999^(i)
        for i in range(len(t)-1):
            val += (t[i+1] - t[i]) * (0.999 ** i)
        return val
    
    # Now, because of symmetry, E[h(theta)] is periodic with period 2*pi/101.
    # The overall expected perimeter is P = \int_0^(2*pi) E[h(phi)] dphi.
    # But due to the rotational symmetry (with period 2*pi/101), the integral over phi is 101 times the integral over a fundamental domain [0, 2*pi/101].
    
    # We'll perform numerical integration over theta in [0, 2*pi/101] using Simpson or trapezoidal rule with N steps.
    
    N = 10000
    a = 0.0
    b = 2*math.pi/101
    h_vals = []
    thetas = [a + i*(b-a)/N for i in range(N+1)]
    for theta in thetas:
        thr = thresholds(theta)
        h_vals.append(E_h(theta, thr))
    # Use Simpson's rule
    s = 0.0
    for i in range(0, N+1):
        weight = 4 if i % 2 == 1 else 2
        if i==0 or i==N:
            weight = 1
        s += weight * h_vals[i]
    integral = (b - a) * s / (3 * N)
    perimeter = 101 * integral
    # Multiply by 1e9 and take floor
    result = int(math.floor(1e9 * perimeter))
    return result

if __name__ == '__main__':
    print(answer())

Let $n=10^{12}$, and note that $W$ restricts to a function from $S_n$ to itself, where $S_n$ is the symmetric group of order $n$. Let $\sigma$ be a permutation chosen uniformly at random from $S_n$, and let $X$ be the expected number of integers $1\le i<n$ such that $F(\sigma)(i+1)<F(\sigma)(i)$. Compute $\lfloor X\rfloor$.

Let $M_{1000}^4$ be the set of $4$-tuples of invertible $1000\times1000$ matrices with coefficients in $\mathbb{C}$. Let $S\subset M_{1000}^4$ be the subset of all tuples $(A_1,A_2,A_3,A_4)$ satisfying the conditions:
\begin{alignat*}{2}
A_i^2 &= I, &&\quad \text{for all } 1\le i\le 4\\
A_iA_j &= A_jA_i, &&\quad \text{if } \{3j- i, 3i-j\} \cap 5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} =\emptyset \\
A_iA_jA_i^{-1}A_j^{-1} &= A_jA_i, &&\quad \text{if } \{3j- i, 3i-j\} \cap 5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} \neq\emptyset,

where $5\mathbb{Z}_{>0}$ refers to the set of positive multiples of $5$, i.e., $5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} = \{5,10,15,\dots\}$.

\[B\cdot (A_1,A_2,A_3,A_4) = (BA_1B^{-1}, BA_2B^{-1}, BA_3B^{-1}, BA_4B^{-1}).\]
Find the number of orbits of this action, i.e., find $|S/G|$.

Let $\mathcal P$ be a regular $101$-gon of circumradius $1$. Draw each diagonal of $\mathcal P$ with probability 0.001. This splits $\mathcal P$ into several closed regions. Let $E$ be the expected value of the perimeter of the region containing the center of $\mathcal P$.

Compute $\lfloor 10^9 E \rfloor.$

\textit{Definitions.} For a positive integer $n$, let $v_p(n)$ denote the largest integer $v$ such that $p^v \mid n$.

$$\ord_{p, x}(a) = \prod_{q \le x} q^{v_q(\ord_p(a))} \prod_{q > x} q^{v_q(p-1)}.$$

$$\ord_{p, x}(2) > \ord_{p, x}(3),$$

$$d_x := \frac{|S_x|}{|\{p \le x : p \text{ is prime}|\}}$$

The \emph{Tsirelson space} is a Banach space introduced in 1974 as a counterexample to a longstanding conjecture in Banach space theory. It is a subspace of $\ell^\infty$ described as follows. 

Let $P_k$ denote the operator on $\ell^\infty$ that modifies each $\langle x_n:\, n \in \mathbb N \rangle \in \ell^\infty$ by setting the first $k$ coordinates equal to $0$ (without changing any other coordinates). Given $\vec x = \langle x_n:\, n \in \mathbb N \rangle \in \ell^\infty$, the \emph{support} of $\vec x$ is $\mathrm{supp}(\vec x) = \{ n \in \mathbb N :\, x_n \neq 0 \}$. 
The Tsirelson space is the span of the smallest pointwise-closed subset $K$ of the unit ball in $\ell^\infty$ satisfying the following two properties:

1. For every $j \in \mathbb N$, the unit vector $\vec e_j$ and all its multiples $\lambda \vec e_j$ with $|\lambda| \leq 1$ belong to $K$.

2. Suppose $\vec x_1,\dots,\vec x_k$ is a finite sequence of members of $K$, each with finite support, such that $\max (\mathrm{supp}(\vec x_i)) < \min (\mathrm{supp}(\vec x_j))$ for all $i < j \leq k$. Then $\frac{1}{2}P_k(\vec x_1 + \dots + \vec x_k) \in K$.

\textit{Problem.}

Let $N$ denote the largest natural number such that 
there is a member $\langle x_n:\, n \in \mathbb N \rangle$ of the set $K$ described above with 
$x_3 = 1/2$, $x_N > 1/20$, and 
$x_i > x_j$ whenever $3 \leq i < j \leq N$. 
It turns out that $N$ is too large to write down in a reasonable amount of space, so let us ask instead: what is the largest power of $2$ dividing $N$? In other words, find the integer $p \geq 0$ such that $N = m2^p$ for some odd number $m$.

Let $W$ be the set of finite words with all distinct letters over the alphabet of positive integers. Define a function $F:W\rightarrow W$ recursively as follows. First, let $F(\epsilon)=\epsilon$, where $\epsilon$ is the empty word. Given a nonempty word $w\in W$, let $F(w)=F(L)F(R)m$, where $m$ is the largest number in $w$ and $w=LmR$. Let $n=10^{12}$, and note that $W$ restricts to a function from $S_n$ to itself, where $S_n$ is the symmetric group of order $n$. Let $\sigma$ be a permutation chosen uniformly at random from $S_n$, and let $X$ be the expected number of integers $1\le i<n$ such that $F(\sigma)(i+1)<F(\sigma)(i)$. Compute $\lfloor X\rfloor$.

Let $M_{1000}^4$ be the set of $4$-tuples of invertible $1000\times1000$ matrices with coefficients in $\mathbb{C}$. Let $S\subset M_{1000}^4$ be the subset of all tuples $(A_1,A_2,A_3,A_4)$ satisfying the conditions:
\begin{alignat*}{2}
A_i^2 &= I, &&\quad \text{for all } 1\le i\le 4\\
A_iA_j &= A_jA_i, &&\quad \text{if } \{3j- i, 3i-j\} \cap 5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} =\emptyset \\
A_iA_jA_i^{-1}A_j^{-1} &= A_jA_i, &&\quad \text{if } \{3j- i, 3i-j\} \cap 5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} \neq\emptyset,
\end{alignat*}
where $5\mathbb{Z}_{>0}$ refers to the set of positive multiples of $5$, i.e., $5\mathbb{Z}_{>0} = \{5,10,15,\dots\}$.

\noindent The group $G = GL(1000)$ of invertible complex $1000\times 1000$ matrices acts on $S$ by the formula:
\[B\cdot (A_1,A_2,A_3,A_4) = (BA_1B^{-1}, BA_2B^{-1}, BA_3B^{-1}, BA_4B^{-1}).\]
Find the number of orbits of this action, i.e., find $|S/G|$.

Let $\mathcal P$ be a regular $101$-gon of circumradius $1$. Draw each diagonal of $\mathcal P$ with probability 0.001. This splits $\mathcal P$ into several closed regions. Let $E$ be the expected value of the perimeter of the region containing the center of $\mathcal P$.

Compute $\lfloor 10^9 E \rfloor.$

Construct a degree 19 polynomial $p(x) \in \mathbb{C}[x]$ such that $X:=\{p(x) = p(y)\} \subset \mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1$ has at least 3 (but not all linear) irreducible components over $\mathbb{C}$. Choose $p(x)$ to be odd, monic, have real coefficients and linear coefficient -19 and calculate $p(19)$.

\textit{Definitions.} For a positive integer $n$, let $v_p(n)$ denote the largest integer $v$ such that $p^v \mid n$.

For $p$ a prime and $a \not\equiv 0 \pmod{p}$, we let $\ord_p(a)$ denote the smallest positive integer $o$ such that $a^o \equiv 1 \pmod{p}$. For $x > 0$, we let
$$\ord_{p, x}(a) = \prod_{q \le x} q^{v_q(\ord_p(a))} \prod_{q > x} q^{v_q(p-1)}.$$

\textit{Problem.} Let $S_x$ denote the set of primes $p$ for which
$$\ord_{p, x}(2) > \ord_{p, x}(3),$$
and let $d_x$ denote the density
$$d_x := \frac{|S_x|}{|\{p \le x : p \text{ is prime}|\}}$$
of $S_x$ in the primes. Let
$$d_{\infty} = \lim_{x \to \infty} d_x.$$

Compute $\lfloor 10^6 d_{\infty} \rfloor.$

How many nonzero points are there on
\[x^3y+y^3z+z^3x=0\]

over $\mathbb{F}_{5^{18}}$ up to scaling?

Let $a_n$ for $n\in\mathbb Z$ be the sequence of integers satisfying the recurrence formula 
   \[a_n=198130309625 a_{n-1}+354973292077 a_{n-2}-427761277677 a_{n-3}+370639957a_{n-4}\]
    with initial conditions $a_i=i$ for $0\leq i\leq 3$. Find the smallest prime $p\equiv4\bmod7$ for which the function $\mathbb Z\to\mathbb Z$ given by $n\mapsto a_n$ can be extended to a continuous function on $\mathbb Z_p$.

There are unique constants $C, \alpha, \beta$ such that the number of solutions to the equation

$$ab+1=cde$$

with $a, b, c, d, e \in \mathbb{N}$ with $ab \le x$ is asymptotic to $Cx^{\alpha}\log^{\beta}x$ as $x\rightarrow\infty.$

Compute $\lfloor 1000C\rfloor.$

Let $U\subset\mathbb{P}H^0(\mathbb{P}^2_{\mathbb{Z}}, \mathcal{O}(2))$ be the space of smooth conics in $\mathbb{P}^2_{\mathbb{Z}}$, and let
$Z\subset U^6$ be the closed subscheme parametrizing $6$-tuples $(C_1, \cdots, C_6)$ with $C_1$ tangent to $C_2, \cdots, C_6$. Let $\pi: Z\to C^5$
be the map induced by the projection onto the last $5$ coordinates, and let $V\subset U^5$ be the dense open subscheme over which $\pi$ is finite \'etale.
Let $$L=\lim_{p\to \infty} \frac{1}{\#V(\mathbb{F}_p)}\sum_{x\in V(\mathbb{F}_p)} \#\pi^{-1}(x),$$
that is, the limit of the average number of components of the space of conics tangent to 5 smooth conics over $\mathbb{F}_p$, as $p$ tends to infinity (when this space is finite \'etale).
